key_insight: ILLUME+ is a scalable explainability framework that goes beyond single-gene rankings to capture coordinated gene activity behind cancer drug response.
clinical_relevance: By making drug-response predictions more interpretable and biologically grounded, it may help researchers generate hypotheses about resistance and sensitivity mechanisms in precision oncology.
method: Post-hoc explainability integrated into an end-to-end transcriptomic drug-response prediction pipeline
Produces multiple complementary explanation types instead of only univariate gene importance scores
Designed to improve stability, robustness, and biological interpretability at scale
evidence_signal: According to the abstract, ILLUME+ outperforms baseline explainers in stability, recovers known drug-gene associations and mechanisms, and supports novel interaction-driven hypothesis generation.
watch_item: As a post-hoc framework, its usefulness still depends on model quality, data representativeness, and careful validation of any biological hypotheses it suggests.
paper_title: Explainable AI for Cancer Drug Response Prediction: Beyond Univariate Feature Attributions
